notes.text: It isn't a perfect match, but since `[Module V]` is the canonical way to write "vector space" in Mathlib I think this tag is worth adding.
I think we should rather point to https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Module_(mathematics)

triage.reason: Reviewer faenuccio identifies the concrete fix: Mathlib's `Module` formalizes Module (mathematics), not vector space (Q125977 is strictly narrower), so retarget the QID while keeping the declaration.
triage.fix_hint: Retarget QID from Q125977 (vector space) to the Wikidata item for Module (mathematics) — Mathlib's `Module` is over a semiring, strictly more general than a vector space.

triage.reason: Reviewer points to the concrete fix: tag riemannZeta (the Riemann zeta function itself) instead of completedRiemannZeta (the completed/symmetrized version).
triage.fix_hint: Retag Q187235 (Riemann zeta function) onto `riemannZeta` in Mathlib/NumberTheory/LSeries/RiemannZeta.lean; `completedRiemannZeta` is the completed variant and should get its own (different) QID if any.

notes.text: The wikipedia article is completely focused on complex ones, so I would add this tag to https://leanprover-community.github.io/mathlib4_docs/Mathlib/RingTheory/RootsOfUnity/Complex.html#Complex.mem_rootsOfUnity or any of its friends.

triage.reason: Reviewer points to a concrete retargeting: the Wikipedia article focuses on complex roots of unity, so tag the complex-specific declaration instead.
triage.fix_hint: Move the @[wikidata Q756747] tag from rootsOfUnity to Complex.mem_rootsOfUnity (or a sibling in Mathlib/RingTheory/RootsOfUnity/Complex.lean) since the Wikipedia article is complex-specific.
